title: Werauhia nutans
intro: Werauhia nutans, also known as the "Tropical Air Plant," is a type of epiphytic bromeliad native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Werauhia nutans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Werauhia nutans is often prepared as a tea or infused in a tincture, and it is also used in creams and ointments to treat skin conditions, wounds, and burns.
taxonomy: Werauhia nutans is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is a type of epiphyte native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is often classified as part of the genus Werauhia, which is a reclassification from the genus Aechmea. Werauhia nutans is a relatively small plant, typically growing to a height of around 30-60 cm. The exact classification of Werauhia nutans may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Bromeliad family.
uses: Werauhia nutans is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and reducing inflammation. In some cultures, Werauhia nutans is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antifungal activities, suggesting its use in treating infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and uses of Werauhia nutans.

parts: Werauhia nutans is a species of flowering plant, but specific information about its medicinal parts is not readily available.

side_effects: Werauhia nutans is a plant known for its potential side effects when ingested or used as a medicinal treatment. It is said to cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, in some individuals.
